mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity risk premiums and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad consumer demand strength that supports corporate earnings and generates positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.5 over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions indicates capacity constraints that force central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration predicts negative returns in late-cycle regimes but shows positive or zero predictive power in early-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect economic growth that boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 10-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive Fed tightening that compresses equity multiples and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with a rising federal funds rate indicates an overheating economy where monetary policy dominates growth optimism, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 growth and FEDFUNDS levels fails to explain variance in 60-day returns with an adjusted R-squared below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth alongside falling unemployment during post-shock recovery phases signals robust economic normalization that sustains positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal predicts negative returns in post-shock recovery periods or shows no predictive power outside of expansion regimes.
